sections.content:
After moving LEFT:
- Player (N) moved from cols 32-35 to cols 29-31 (moved left by 3 cols)
- White projectile (W) moved from cols 32-35 to cols 28 (a 1x4 vertical strip? boundary shows [[40, 28], [43, 28]] - that's weird, only 2 points)
- The dark gray frame (G) at rows 36-39, cols 32-35 seems to have disappeared (not in the output)
- The blue target at rows 37-38 is still there
Wait, node 9 (W) has boundary [[40, 28], [43, 28]] with only 4 pixels. That's strange - it seems like the white block became a thin vertical strip.
Actually, looking at the image, the player moved left and the white block is now to the left of the player. The dark gray frame at rows 36-39 seems to have disappeared.
Let me think about the game differently. Looking at the image:
- The player (green) moved left
- The white block seems to be trailing or is a separate entity
- The bottom target area now just shows the blue square without the dark frame
Maybe the game is about navigating the player to collect the blue squares. The white block might be a "tail" or "trail" that follows the player.
Let me try to navigate to the left target (blue at cols 17-18, rows 29-30). The player is now at cols 29-31. I need to move left more to get to cols 16-19.
Actually, let me reconsider. Each LEFT move shifts by 3 columns. To go from col 29 to col 17, I need about 4 LEFT moves (12 cols). But I also need to go UP to reach row 29-30.
Let me first get to the left target's column, then move up.

<parameter=code>
# Player at cols 29-31, left target at cols 16-19
# Need to move LEFT ~4 times to align, then UP to reach target row
# Let me move LEFT 4 times to get near the left target column
action(['LEFT', 'LEFT', 'LEFT', 'LEFT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
